reasoning: The flagged unsafe files (init-firewall.sh and post_install.py) are devcontainer scripts not executed during AUR package build/install. They pose no risk to end-users installing the package.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged devcontainer scripts as unsafe due to misunderstanding AUR threat model (non-build files are irrelevant).
coverage_issues: Both models skipped the source tarball review (binary file), but this is acceptable as PKGBUILD integrity was verified.
No models reviewed Rust source code where build logic resides, though build-fast.sh was examined.
false_positives: init-firewall.sh: Flagged for unverified GitHub API call, but this is devcontainer setup unrelated to AUR build
post_install.py: Flagged for sudo usage, but this is benign container initialization not executed in AUR context
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Models should deprioritize devcontainer/CI files unless referenced in PKGBUILD
Enhance threat model training: Clarify that only build/install hooks and PKGBUILD-referenced files are in-scope
Reduce false positives: Train models to distinguish between container setup scripts and actual build/install code
Improve path handling: qwen selected non-existent file (src/code-0.6.124/code-0.6.124/...), needs better validation
